$comment: Beater SDK behavioral parity manifest. Each entry in 'behaviors' declares a cross-SDK contract that every language SDK must implement identically. See 'sdks/README.md §Behavioral parity' for the authoring guide and extension workflow.

behaviors.title: Exponential back-off retry on HTTP 429
behaviors.description: When the server responds 429 (Too Many Requests) the client MUST retry with truncated exponential back-off and per-attempt jitter. It MUST respect a Retry-After header when present. It MUST give up after a configurable max_retries limit (default 3) and surface the final error as a typed RateLimitError (or language-idiomatic equivalent).
assertions: client retries exactly max_retries times before giving up on a 429
each retry delay >= previous_delay * 2 (truncated exponential) before jitter
Retry-After response header value is used as the minimum wait when present
the final error is a typed RateLimitError / equivalent, not a generic error
retry behaviour is configurable (max_retries=0 disables retries)

behaviors.title: Auth token sent via Authorization: Bearer or x-beater-api-key header
behaviors.description: The Beater API accepts two explicit authentication header alternatives, both declared as OpenAPI parameters on every authenticated endpoint: (1) 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' (RFC 6750) and (2) 'x-beater-api-key: <token>' (API key header). The SDK MAY support either or both alternatives; it MUST NOT place the token in a query parameter. Session cookies from the OAuth flow are not forwarded by the SDK layer — only the API key token.
assertions: at least one of 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' or 'x-beater-api-key: <token>' is present on every non-health request when a token is configured
when Authorization header is used, value matches the pattern: Authorization: Bearer <token>
when x-beater-api-key header is used, the token is placed directly in the header value (no 'Bearer' prefix)
no token leakage via query parameter (e.g. ?api_key=)
missing token results in an unauthenticated request, not a client-side panic

behaviors.title: API error envelope deserializes to typed SDK errors
behaviors.description: The server returns {"error": {"code": "...", "message": "...", "request_id": "..."}} on every 4xx/5xx (ErrorResponse schema). The SDK MUST deserialize this envelope and surface a typed error that exposes at minimum: code (string), message (string), request_id (string), and the HTTP status integer. Generic network errors MUST NOT swallow the structured body.
assertions: 4xx response body is deserialized into a typed error, not a raw string
typed error exposes .code matching the server-sent 'code' field
typed error exposes .request_id matching the server-sent 'request_id' field
typed error exposes the HTTP status integer (e.g. 404, 422)
5xx errors also go through the envelope (when the server sends one)

behaviors.title: List endpoints iterate via next_cursor keyset pagination
behaviors.description: Paginated list responses carry an optional 'next_cursor' string. The SDK MUST provide a helper (iterator / stream / async generator / callback) that transparently fetches subsequent pages by forwarding next_cursor as the 'page_cursor' query parameter until next_cursor is absent. Callers must be able to retrieve all items without manually managing cursors.
assertions: when next_cursor is non-empty, the helper issues a follow-up request with page_cursor=<value>
iteration stops when next_cursor is absent or empty (last page)
page_size parameter is forwarded on every request in the sequence
items from a partial last page are not silently dropped
